Rarity Distribution

Breeding Tips

  • 1.Always health test both parents for hip dysplasia, patella, BOAS, and degenerative myelopathy.
  • 2.Never breed Merle to Merle (MM double merles risk deafness/blindness).
  • 3.Dilutes (dd) can develop color dilution alopecia — verify coat quality in bloodlines.
  • 4.Breeding common colors often produces better litter size and maternal health.
  • 5.Rares command premium prices but require established buyer networks.
  • 6.Pairing a carrier (e.g., blue) with a visual of a rarer type maximizes diversity.
